title: Newton's Laws of Motion Unveiled
summary: Discover Isaac Newton’s groundbreaking laws that govern motion and explain their profound impact on physics.

sections.heading: Newton's First Law - The Law of Inertia
sections.body: The first law states an object at rest will stay at rest, and one in motion will remain in motion unless acted upon by a net external force. This is often called 'inertia'. A key application can be seen when you observe objects like frictionless pucks on ice moving smoothly until they strike something.
key_points: Inertia defines resistance to changes in motion

sections.heading: Newton's Second Law - The Relationship Between Force, Mass, and Acceleration
sections.body: The second law introduces the equation F=ma (Force equals mass times acceleration), revealing that heavier objects require more force to change their motion. This concept is vital for understanding why larger vehicles take longer to speed up or stop than smaller ones.
key_points: Mass and acceleration are directly proportional
Greater mass demands greater net force

sections.heading: Newton's Third Law - Action-Reaction Pairs
sections.body: The third law tells us that for every action, there is an equal and opposite reaction. This explains why when you jump off a small boat into the water, it moves in the other direction; your legs push down on the boat (action), and the boat reacts by moving away from you.

key_facts: F=ma is Newton’s Second Law of Motion equation
